      "それはハリケーンよりも低い損耗率と高い撃墜比を特徴としています。",
      "それは愛好家の間で人気があることを特徴としています。",
      "それは戦争中継続的に生産された唯一のイギリス製戦闘機であることを特徴としています。",

      "それは地中海戦域で使用されます。",

      "それは東南アジア戦域で使用されます。",

      "それは1942年から1950年代半ばまで艦隊航空隊で使用されます。",
      "それは航空ショーで使用されます。"

      "それはイギリスにあります。"
    ],

      "それはバーミンガム、キャッスル・ブロムウィッチで建造されました。"

IsA: It is a British single-seat fighter aircraft.
It is a short-range, high-performance interceptor aircraft.
It is an aircraft carrier-based adaptation of the Spitfire.
It is a squadron in the RAF.
HasA: It has a distinctive elliptical wing.
It has innovative sunken rivets.
It has a retractable undercarriage.
It has an enclosed cockpit.
It has oxygen-breathing apparatus.
It has smaller and thinner wings.
It has a Rolls-Royce PV XII V-12 engine.
It has a fine-pitch propeller.
It has a new and better-shaped, two-bladed, wooden propeller.
It has an undercarriage position indicator.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its elliptical wing designed by Beverley Shenstone.
It is characterized by innovative sunken rivets.
It is characterized by a thinnest possible cross-section.
It is characterized by a short-range, high-performance interceptor design.
It is characterized by its adaptability to increasingly powerful engines.
It is characterized by its higher performance compared to the Hawker Hurricane.
It is characterized by a lower attrition rate and higher victory-to-loss ratio than Hurricanes.
It is characterized by its popularity among enthusiasts.
It is characterized by its continuous production throughout the war.
It is characterized by its use in several roles including interceptor, photo-reconnaissance, fighter-bomber, and trainer.
UsedFor: It is used for air defense.
It is used for resisting the Luftwaffe.
It is used in the European theatre.
It is used in the Mediterranean theatre.
It is used in the Pacific theatre.
It is used in the South-East Asian theatre.
It is used in several roles including interceptor, photo-reconnaissance, fighter-bomber, and trainer.
It is used until the 1950s.
It is used in the Fleet Air Arm from 1942 until the mid-1950s.
It is used at air shows.
BuiltIn: It was built in Woolston, Southampton.
It was built in Castle Bromwich, Birmingham.
BuiltBy: It was built by Supermarine Aviation Works.
OriginatedIn: It originated in the need for a modern fighter to replace the Gloster Gauntlet biplane.
It originated in specification F7/30 released by the Air Ministry in 1931.
It originated in R. J. Mitchell's design of the Supermarine Type 224.
It originated in the experience with the Schneider Trophy seaplanes.
It originated in the Type 300 design.
CreatedBy: It was created by R. J. Mitchell.
It was created by Joseph Smith.
CreatedIn: It was created in Supermarine Aviation Works.
DevelopedBy: It was developed by R. J. Mitchell.
It was developed by Joseph Smith.
DevelopedIn: It was developed in Supermarine Aviation Works.
ManufacturedBy: It was manufactured by Morris Motors Limited.
It was manufactured by Castle Bromwich Aircraft Factory (CBAF).
ConsistsOf: It consists of a Rolls-Royce Merlin engine.
It consists of Rolls-Royce Griffon engines.
It consists of several wing configurations.
PartOf: It is part of the Royal Air Force.
It is part of Allied countries' air forces.
It is part of the Fleet Air Arm.
It is part of the Battle of Britain Memorial Flight.
KnownFor: It is known for being a British single-seat fighter aircraft used during World War II.
It is known for its distinctive elliptical wing.
It is known for its high performance.
It is known for capturing the public's imagination during the Battle of Britain.
It is known for its lower attrition rate and higher victory-to-loss ratio than Hurricanes.
It is known for its continued service until the 1950s.
It is known for its popularity among enthusiasts.
It is known for being the only British fighter produced continuously throughout the war.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with World War II.
It is associated with the Royal Air Force.
It is associated with R. J. Mitchell.
It is associated with Beverley Shenstone.
It is associated with Joseph Smith.
It is associated with Rolls-Royce Merlin engine.
It is associated with Rolls-Royce Griffon engine.
It is associated with the Battle of Britain.
It is associated with the Hawker Hurricane.
It is associated with the Messerschmitt Bf 109E-series aircraft.
OccurredIn: It occurred in World War II.
It occurred during the Battle of Britain.
It occurred in the European theatre.
It occurred in the Mediterranean theatre.
It occurred in the Pacific theatre.
It occurred in the South-East Asian theatre.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the Schneider Trophy seaplanes.
It was influenced by specification F7/30.
It was influenced by specification F10/35.
It was influenced by the Hawker Hurricane.
It was influenced by the Messerschmitt Bf 109E-series aircraft.
Influenced: It influenced the design of subsequent fighter aircraft.
It influenced the public's imagination during the Battle of Britain.
It influenced the RAF's choice of principal aircraft after the Battle of Britain.
